page: Microsoft Azure F8s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FSv2 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor built on the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory capacity is 16.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance includes 66 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations place the F8s_v2 consistently in the average performance tier across most compute and memory metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1409.09, and the multi-core score is 7431.92. Memory bandwidth tests show a cached read-only small block speed of 415.54 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reach 47.24 GB/sec and 35.21 GB/sec respectively. In application-specific tests, the server achieves 6.98 million operations per second on Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 6.4 GB/sec.

A notable performance tradeoff is observed in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, which scored 27.63 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested servers. However, single-threaded operations remain in the average tier, with gzip compression reaching 24.03 GB/sec. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the F8s_v2 offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio for workloads requiring dedicated Intel Xeon processing power without the overhead of high memory allocations.

This server configuration is suitable for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and entry-level database operations. Its support for AVX and AVX-512 instructions also makes it applicable for vector-based mathematical computations and light machine learning inference, as demonstrated by its average prompt processing speed of 447.5 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter model.
description: Microsoft Azure F8s_v2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers average performance across most benchmarks, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 7431.92 and a static web serving throughput of 6.4 GB/sec. A performance tradeoff is present in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, which falls into the bottom 25% tier. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy tasks that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is suitable for web serving, software compilation, and light vector-based mathematical workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8s_v2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 66 GB local storage, delivering average performance for web serving and compilation.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced resource profile for web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
16.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
Supports AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets for vector calculations
Multi-threaded decompression performance is relatively weak compared to compute capacity
